source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design a welded multi-body Tier I commuter rail car end-beam + collision/corner post assembly per 49 CFR 238 Subpart C, consisting of: (A) one end beam 250 x 200 mm box section spanning the full 3.05 m (10 ft) car width; (B) two collision posts 200 x 150 mm section x 2.6 m tall on the longitudinal centerlines of the draft gear; (C) two corner posts 150 x 150 mm section x 2.6 m tall at the outer corners; (D) a family of full-penetration + fillet welds (AWS D1.1 qualified) joining the end beam, posts, and the underframe. All primary members are A572 Grade 50 structural steel plate (yield 345 MPa, UTS 450 MPa, Young's modulus 200 GPa, density 7850 kg/m^3, Charpy minimum 20 J at -40 C per FRA guidance).

Load cases per 49 CFR 238.203 / 238.205 / 238.211 / 238.213: LC1 buff 800,000 lbf (3.56 MN) compressive along the draft-gear centerline distributed across the end beam and collision posts; LC2 anti-telescoping vertical 500,000 lbf (2.22 MN) at the anti-climber; LC3 collision post ultimate 300,000 lbf (1.33 MN) applied 18 in (457 mm) above underframe; LC4 collision post intermediate 500,000 lbf (2.22 MN) at 30 in (762 mm) above underframe; LC5 corner post ultimate 150,000 lbf (667 kN) at 18 in; LC6 corner post intermediate 300,000 lbf (1.33 MN) at 30 in; LC7 revenue-service fatigue spectrum per APTA PR-CS-S-034-99 for 30-year life.

Pass/fail on the assembled model per 49 CFR 238.203/205/211/213 + AAR Manual Standard S-034 + AWS D1.1: (R1) LC1 buff max von Mises on all primary members <= yield 345 MPa with no permanent deformation; (R2) LC2 max von Mises <= UTS 450 MPa; (R3) LC3 max von Mises <= UTS 450 MPa; (R4) LC4 <= yield 345 MPa; (R5) LC5 <= UTS 450 MPa; (R6) LC6 <= yield 345 MPa; (R7) fatigue weld detail classification Category B per AAR S-034 for 30-year life; (R8) weld procedure qualified to AWS D1.1 with Charpy >= 20 J at -40 C per FRA guidance; (R_asm1) weld-throat DCR (stress range on each welded joint normalized by Category B S-N allowable) <= 1.0 under LC7 fatigue, and under LC1-LC6 each weld throat utilization <= 1.0 on yield / UTS limit that governs the attached member.

Manufacturing: plate laser / plasma cut to size; welded by qualified AWS D1.1 procedure with minimum 20 J Charpy at -40 C; full-penetration butt welds at end-beam-to-post connections; fillet welds at gusset attachments; car-body-to-underframe welds per AAR S-034. The final FEM check runs on the fully assembled model (end beam + 2 collision posts + 2 corner posts + explicit weld throats) with shell elements in member bodies and solid elements at weld details. Per-member stress is reported but assembly-level passing requires all CFR load-case limits, the Category B fatigue classification, and the weld-throat DCR envelope to hold simultaneously.

Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the end-beam / corner-post weldment with weld callouts; FEA report across all seven load cases; fatigue analysis per AAR S-034; weld procedure specifications per AWS D1.1.
short_prompt:
Design a welded multi-body Tier I commuter rail car end-beam + collision/corner post assembly per 49 CFR 238 Subpart C. The end beam is a 250 x 200 mm box section spanning the full 3.05 m (10 ft) car width; two collision posts are 200 x 150 mm x 2.6 m tall on the draft-gear longitudinal centerlines; two corner posts are 150 x 150 mm x 2.6 m tall at the outer corners; AWS D1.1-qualified full-penetration + fillet welds join the family to the underframe. Primary members are A572 Grade 50 (yield 345 MPa, UTS 450 MPa, E = 200 GPa, rho = 7850 kg/m^3, Charpy >= 20 J at -40 C).

Load cases per 49 CFR 238.203/205/211/213.
- LC1 buff: 800000 lbf (3.56 MN) compressive along draft-gear centerline.
- LC2 anti-telescoping vertical: 500000 lbf (2.22 MN) at anti-climber.
- LC3 collision post ultimate: 300000 lbf (1.33 MN) at 18 in (457 mm) above underframe.
- LC4 collision post intermediate: 500000 lbf (2.22 MN) at 30 in (762 mm) above underframe.
- LC5 corner post ultimate: 150000 lbf (667 kN) at 18 in.
- LC6 corner post intermediate: 300000 lbf (1.33 MN) at 30 in.
- LC7 revenue-service fatigue spectrum per APTA PR-CS-S-034-99 for 30-year life.

Governing limits: LC1, LC4, LC6 <= yield 345 MPa; LC2, LC3, LC5 <= UTS 450 MPa; LC7 weld detail Category B per AAR S-034 for 30-year life.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: AWS D1.1 + AAR S-034 Category weld fatigue check is a closed-form S-N based calculation.

prompt.title: FRA Tier I commuter rail car end-beam + collision/corner post assembly (multi-part)
prompt.brief: Multi-body welded car-end: one end beam + 2 collision posts + 2 corner posts + qualified AWS D1.1 welds, all A572 Gr 50. Must sustain 800,000 lbf buff, collision post 300,000 lbf, corner post 150,000 lbf per 49 CFR 238 Subpart C plus 30-year APTA PR-CS-S-034-99 fatigue. Final FEM check runs on the assembled weldment.

parts.name: end_beam
parts.role: 250 x 200 mm box end beam spanning the full car width, carries buff + anti-telescoping load

parts.name: collision_post
parts.role: 200 x 150 mm collision post 2.6 m tall on draft-gear centerline, carries CFR 238.211 collision loads

parts.name: corner_post
parts.role: 150 x 150 mm corner post 2.6 m tall at outer corners, carries CFR 238.213 corner loads

parts.name: welded_connection_family
parts.role: AWS D1.1-qualified full-penetration + fillet welds joining end beam, collision posts, corner posts, and underframe; governs fatigue per AAR S-034 Category B

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 3.05 m (car width) x 0.25 m (end-beam depth) x 2.6 m (post height above underframe)
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: Underframe in XY plane; car longitudinal axis along X; posts along Z

boundary_conditions.car_body_underframe: end-beam assembly welded to underframe, considered fixed at underframe
boundary_conditions.inter_part_fastener_stiffness: AWS D1.1-qualified full-penetration + fillet welds at end-beam-to-post and post-to-underframe joints

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: AAR S-034 weld category B for 30-year revenue life
FRA guidance for Tier I cold climate
assembly-scope DCR: weld-throat stress range vs Category B S-N under LC7 and weld-throat utilization vs governing member yield / UTS under LC1-LC6
Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

requirements.governing_standard: 49 CFR 238 Subpart C + APTA PR-CS-S-034-99 + AAR Manual Standard S-034 + AWS D1.1

analysis_types: linear static x6 + weld fatigue S-N check

interfaces.load_path: collision post welded to end beam with full-penetration butt + fillet reinforcement; transmits axial + 300 kip ultimate + 500 kip intermediate loads
corner post welded to end beam; transmits 150 kip ultimate + 300 kip intermediate corner loads
end beam welded to car-body underframe at fixed boundary; transmits buff + anti-telescoping load
collision post base welded to underframe; governed by AAR S-034 Category B fatigue
corner post base welded to underframe; governed by AAR S-034 Category B fatigue

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: A Tier I rail car-end is fundamentally a multi-body weldment (end beam + 2 collision posts + 2 corner posts + underframe welds) whose CFR pass/fail is evaluated on the integrated structure; single-body lumped models cannot resolve per-member CFR limits or Category B weld fatigue.
notes.speculation_caveats: Member counts (1 end beam, 2 collision posts, 2 corner posts) are dictated by 49 CFR 238 Subpart C topology; weld detail family is speculated as a distinct part_family to carry the AAR S-034 fatigue check.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
